utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with the required details: - health_coverage: specifies provider, coverage description, and both monthly and annual costs. Meets 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'—mental health is not explicitly mentioned, but 'health_coverage' with costs is provided. Given format asks for health insurance with costs; the plan includes costs, so count as covered. - property_coverage: includes provider, coverage description, and costs (monthly and annual). Satisfies 'home/contents insurance with costs.' - travel_coverage: includes provider, coverage description, and costs (monthly and annual). Satisfies 'travel insurance with costs.' - additional_services: included with provider 'None', coverage explanation, and costs €0; acceptable as it still specifies the item with costs. - total_budget: includes monthly_total, annual_total, breakdown, and dates; satisfies 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown.' Therefore, all 5 items are included and specified with costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three providers: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health_coverage), HomeFirst Standard Coverage (property_coverage), and PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel_coverage). I located each in the ground-truth ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan under primary_health_coverage.physical_health has rating 9; HomeFirst Standard Coverage under property_protection.home_content has rating 9; PrimeTravel Premium Plan under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance has rating 3. No additional services were selected, so no mapping needed there.
